Sunil Bharti Mittal says now is the time to ‘double down’ on investing in India: 'Cut obsession with gold imports'
india_trends.summary: Amid soaring global crude oil prices and an energy crisis fuelled by the ongoing US-Iran conflict, Sunil Bharti Mittal, also advocated the use of renewable energy.

india_trends.title: Indian Hotels Q4 Results: Net profit rises 15% YoY to ₹600 crore; announces dividend of ₹3.25 per share
india_trends.summary: Indian Hotels Company reported a Q4FY26 net profit of ₹600 crore, a 15% YoY increase. Revenue rose 14% to ₹2,845 crore, driven by strong hotel segment performance. The company anticipates opening 60 new hotels in FY27, with optimistic demand forecasts.

Wall Street upside? HSBC sees S&P 500 at 8,000, raises 2026 target as AI boom and tech earnings fuel optimism
india_trends.summary: HSBC raised its 2026 S&P 500 target to 7,650, citing strong corporate earnings and resilient technology stocks. The brokerage said improving AI adoption, broader market participation and easing macro conditions could potentially push the index beyond the 8,000 mark despite lingering inflation an

india_trends.title: Govt mulls new programme to bolster rural industrial landscape
india_trends.summary: A new government program, Rural Prosperity and Rural Resilience, will connect industries directly with rural producers. This initiative aims to boost value chains, skills, and market access. It will foster distributed manufacturing and corporate presence in rural areas. The program seeks to transfor

india_trends.title: Govt weighs emergency steps to protect forex reserves
india_trends.summary: India is considering emergency steps to shore up foreign-exchange reserves, including curbing non-essential imports like gold and electronic goods, and hiking fuel prices, to help cushion the economy from the fallout of the Iran war, people familiar with the matter said.

india_trends.title: India considering emergency measures to save foreign exchange
india_trends.summary: India is exploring emergency measures to protect its foreign exchange reserves. These steps may include limiting imports of gold and electronics, and increasing fuel prices. Officials are concerned about rising oil costs and their impact on the economy. The government is also urging citizens to cons

india_trends.title: BSE launches F&O trading on IT index; debut day turnover at Rs 148 crore
india_trends.summary: BSE launched India’s first futures and options contracts linked to the Focused IT Index, offering investors new hedging and trading opportunities in the technology sector. The launch attracted strong participation, reflecting growing interest in sector-specific derivatives amid evolving market dynam

india_trends.title: US stocks today: US stocks slip at open as US-Iran impasse lifts oil price
india_trends.summary: Wall ​Street's ​main indexes opened lower ​on Monday, after last week's record-setting ‌rally, ⁠as renewed ⁠worries over ​stalled U.S.-Iran talks lifted ​oil prices and tempered risk appetite.

india_trends.title: India has 60 days of crude oil, 60 days of Natural Gas & 45 days of LPG rolling stock: Centre
india_trends.summary: India is launching a national mission to conserve fuel amid global energy market turmoil. The government assures citizens of robust energy security and ample reserves. This collective effort aims to ease the nation's financial burden from high international crude prices. Prime Minister Modi urges pu

india_trends.title: FTAs and industrial policy must move in tandem to boost growth, says commerce secretary
india_trends.summary: Rajesh Agarwal calls for aligned trade and industrial strategies to fix the export-import gap, while the DPIIT prepares a global ‘Made in India’ branding push.

india_trends.title: South Indian Bank's gold loan growth to slow on new RBI norms, volatile prices
india_trends.summary: The lender said tighter end-use rules and stricter loan-to-value guidelines could curb growth in its fast-expanding gold loan portfolio despite strong demand.

india_trends.title: Sensex, Nifty outlook for tomorrow: What's next after today's sharp drop? - Business Today
india_trends.summary: Sensex, Nifty outlook for tomorrow: What's next after today's sharp drop?    Business Today

india_trends.title: Commodity Radar: Gold choppy ahead of US inflation data. Sell on rise for these targets?
india_trends.summary: Gold prices are expected to remain volatile with a mild downside bias this week as traders monitor US inflation data, Donald Trump’s China visit and US-Iran talks. Technical indicators suggest cautious sentiment, while analysts recommend a sell-on-rise strategy amid resistance near higher levels.

india_trends.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures steady after Trump rejects Iran proposal; crude spikes
india_trends.summary: US stock futures showed a cautious opening on 11 May, with S&amp;P 500 futures down 0.1% and Dow futures down 0.2%. Investors are monitoring Middle East tensions and awaiting US inflation data, while a rebound in crude oil prices raises concerns about market volatility.

india_trends.title: Market likely to enter recovery phase; this could be right time to invest in Nifty 50 index funds, say experts
india_trends.summary: As the Indian equity market shows signs of recovery, experts recommend investing in Nifty 50 index funds for long-term gains. These funds offer diversification, low costs, and exposure to top companies, making them a strategic choice for investors looking to benefit from market trends.

india_trends.title: Manipal Health acquires land, hospital in Mumbai’s Andheri for Rs 495 crore
india_trends.summary: Manipal Health Enterprises has made headlines by purchasing a hospital site in Andheri, Mumbai for Rs 495 crore. This acquisition is a slice of a broader Rs 908 crore deal, signaling a robust appetite for healthcare real estate among investors in major Indian cities.

india_trends.title: Identifying 100 products to boost domestic manufacturing: DPIIT Secy
india_trends.summary: India is boosting domestic manufacturing. The government is identifying 100 products, including auto parts, for increased local production. A 'Made in India Brand Scheme' will soon launch to assure quality. Artificial intelligence is transforming manufacturing. Businesses are encouraged to invest lo

india_trends.title: Can India withstand extreme energy demand shocks? Shaktikanta Das responds
india_trends.summary: India can now better handle extreme energy demand shocks. Businesses are urged to reduce reliance on single sources and focus on long-term resilience. Strategic reorientation is needed due to global supply chain disruptions. India's infrastructure expansion and renewable energy push strengthen its e
